title: Suspicious Login Activity
description: A student account shows unexpected logins from unfamiliar locations. Reconstruct the browser timeline to identify when and how the sessions occurred.
investigationObjective: Learn to identify authentication artefacts (session cookies) and recognise the temporal patterns of credential compromise by sequencing login, session creation, and password reset events.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://www.bbc.com/news (title: BBC News – Home, referrer: https://www.google.com/)
events.explanation: Unrelated news browsing activity at the start of the session.
Personal weather search — unrelated to any authentication or account activity.

events.description: [Chrome] Google search: "westfield university student portal login"
events.explanation: The user searched for the official university login page, establishing intent to access the portal. This is the logical starting point of the credential sequence.

relationships.label: A search for a login page typically precedes visiting that page.
Searching for a portal often precedes session cookie creation.

hints: Think about which action usually happens first when someone wants to reach a specific website.
Search activities often occur before visits to the discovered page.
The search event should be placed before the history visit to the login page that follows it.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://portal.westfield-uni.ac.uk/login (title: Student Portal – Sign In, User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 Chrome/130.0)
events.explanation: The search result led directly to the university authentication page, placing the user at the credential entry point.

relationships.label: Loading a login page commonly precedes session cookie creation.
Session storage entries are often written during or after the login page load.

hints: Think about what normally happens once a user clicks a search result.
A visit to a login page usually comes before related session artefacts appear.
The history visit to the portal login page should be placed immediately after the Google search and before the PHPSESSID cookie is set.

events.description: [Chrome] Cookie set: PHPSESSID=stu_a8f3c2d1 on portal.westfield-uni.ac.uk (HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, expires: session, Path=/)
events.explanation: A session cookie was issued by the server after the login page loaded, confirming an active browser session with the portal was established.

relationships.label: Session cookies are set by the server after the login page completes loading.
Successful authentication usually redirects the user to an authenticated area.

hints: Think about which artefact is created when a login page finishes loading.
Session cookies are commonly issued after the server validates credentials.
The PHPSESSID cookie should be placed immediately after the portal login visit and before the dashboard access.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ (title: Never Gonna Give You Up, referrer: https://www.youtube.com/)
events.explanation: Unrelated video streaming activity — a known Rickroll URL visited directly via YouTube navigation.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://portal.westfield-uni.ac.uk/dashboard (title: Student Dashboard, authenticated, referrer: https://portal.westfield-uni.ac.uk/login)
events.explanation: Successful authentication redirected the browser to the student dashboard, confirming the session is now authenticated.

relationships.label: A password reset search hours later suggests account state changed after initial dashboard access.

hints: Think about where a user is taken after a login succeeds.
Dashboard access indicates an authenticated session has already been established.
The dashboard visit should be placed right after the session cookie and before the later password reset search.

events.description: [Chrome] Google search: "best coffee shops near westfield campus"
events.explanation: Unrelated local search for coffee shops during an afternoon break — normal browsing noise.

events.description: [Chrome] Google search: "westfield university password reset forgot account"
events.explanation: Hours after the original login, a password reset search suggests either the account owner lost access or an unauthorised party began a credential recovery attempt.

relationships.label: A password reset search typically precedes opening the reset flow page.

hints: Think about why a user might revisit credential recovery options hours after logging in.
A password reset search often reflects a changed account state or lost access.
The password reset search should be placed before the reset page visit and before the auth_token cookie update.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://portal.westfield-uni.ac.uk/account/reset-password (title: Reset Password, referrer: Google, IP: 185.234.72.14)
events.explanation: The browser navigated to the self-service password reset flow. The referral from Google and the external IP address are notable forensic indicators of an atypical access path.

relationships.label: Visiting a reset page usually precedes evidence of a successful reset or re-authentication.

hints: Think about which page follows a user clicking a password reset search result.
Password reset pages are reached after selecting a recovery option from search results.
The reset page visit should be placed right after the reset search and immediately before the persistent auth_token cookie is created.

events.description: [Chrome] Cookie updated: auth_token on portal.westfield-uni.ac.uk (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, expires: 2024-11-15T14:08:55Z, Path=/)
events.explanation: A persistent authentication token was written, confirming the password reset completed successfully. This token supersedes the original session and grants extended access.

relationships.label: A persistent authentication token is typically issued after a reset flow completes.

hints: Think about which artefact is produced when a reset flow finishes successfully.
New or updated persistent auth cookies are strong indicators of a completed authentication change.
The auth_token cookie should be placed last, immediately after the reset page visit.

events.explanation: Unrelated shopping search activity after completing the password reset — normal browsing behaviour.
Unrelated email checking activity — common personal browsing unrelated to the portal investigation.
